employmentRate5064.note: Employment rate for those aged 50 to 64, UK. Labour Force Survey.

inactivityLongTermIllness.note: People aged 50-64 economically inactive due to long-term sickness or disability, UK. Labour Force Survey.

employmentRate65plus.note: Employment rate for those aged 65 and over, UK. Labour Force Survey.

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Labour Force Survey — employment by age group

sources.name: DWP
sources.dataset: Older workers and the labour market

metadata.methodology: Employment rates from ONS Labour Force Survey, seasonally adjusted. Economic inactivity figures by reason from the same survey. Long-term illness figures include both those who say their main reason for inactivity is long-term sickness and those who cite disability.
knownIssues: LFS sample sizes for older age groups are smaller — confidence intervals wider than for prime-age workers
Long-term illness categorisation changed in 2019 — pre and post 2019 not directly comparable
State Pension Age changes since 2010 directly affect 50-64 employment rate as women formerly below SPA are now counted as working-age
